item.title: Loopholes!
item.content:encoded:
I looked this up after reading your "totally normal IKEA" skip the other day. By the way, I mean what I said about the IKEA: it's one of my new favorites. Absolutely brilliant :-)
this one, on the other hand… It's pretty good, and I love the idea in theory, but it feels incomplete somehow. I know skips don't normally spell out all the details (that's kind of the point here: we don't know everything about how/why it works), but I feel like there should've been more hints as to why the anomalous activity started happening after the mayor was elected. It's pretty clear he didn't know why it happened, and if there were any specific clues to the root cause, I missed them.

My negative criticism out of the way, here's things I liked about this:
first, I love how the recovery team managed to abuse loopholes like radio and ESPECIALLY the whole "I'm just going to have a conversation with my subordinate" thing. It's a example of something I call the loophole effect: no matter how carefully and specifically you word something like a law or command, one or more of the recipients of that directive will find a loophole. If you find an antique oil lamp, it's probably best not to rub it at all. Not every genie is as benevolent as Robin Williams ;-)
Second, I love how it portrays the mayor as well-intentioned but misguided. As much as people like to hate on politicians, I like to believe that most of them do genuinely mean well. The problem is that meaning well and doing well are two completely different things. Even if they could warp reality like this guy, where would that lead?
All in all, good skip; I like that it touches on an important issue (even if that wasn't really the goal), just wish you didn't leave quite as much out in terms of explanation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Enjoyed this, well written, overall good skip.

+1

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that a recent edit was reverted, as it was unnecessary. UK spellings of words that use the letter s instead of z are considered a stylistic choice by the author.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Imagine if some con artist would come into this town and get the mayor to sign a law with fine text removing the mayor from power and giving control over to himself.

And then signed a law expanding the town borders to cover the planet.

Mr. Con Artist is now a god.

This SCP had the potential to be REALLY DANGEROUS. It's quite fortunate it self-destructed in a poof of logic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This reminds me of The Twilight Zone, I dig it!
Would love to know what happened to MTF9, and if they and the town were outright erased from existence, or if they actually ended up somewhere else, but I guess that'll just be a mystery

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can imagine the town being sentient like:
"Ok just gotta remove all these military guys…but wait it says here no one can leave…but it says here these military guys have to leave…but it says here OH FU-"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe it's just because I finished Under the Dome recently, but I got some Stephen King vibes from this. Good ones, as well. +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this skip, but a few minor nitpicks: unless the mayor is British, he'd say "main street" not "high street", and "recall election" (or "impeachment") instead of "vote of no confidence". And usually cities/towns have a chief of police, not a sheriff (counties have sheriffs).

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
I really like this skip a lot. I love the thoroughness of the story, and I think the characterization of both Stepper-1 and the Mayor are done well too.

Anyway, here's wonderwall:
https://youtu.be/eSn5PhXpDeA

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great work on this skip. I can tell a lot of effort went into it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Man, that mayor could have done a LOT worse.
"The town of Cullen now encompasses the entire Universe."
Bam, suddenly the mayor is God.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I got the feeling that he cares far more about the town itself as an entity (and especially how it is perceived by others) than about the people themselves.

Cullen is a good town, dammit, and I won't have it ruined by the ungrateful attitude of people afraid of a hard day's work!

He says the town is good, then goes on to talk about how (a presumably large portion of) its populace is a bunch of lazy lay-abouts who don't want to help achieve his vision of small-town perfection. It's obviously their failing, and not his or, heaven forbid, a failing of the town.

Genshed mentioned getting wished into the corn field, and that Twilight Zone episode is a good parallel. He doesn't care if the townspeople are actually happy, as long as they play along and pretend that they are happy and agree with him that the town is the best place to live ever.

EDIT: Also relevant: the road to Hell and the paving materials thereof.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wasted opportunity. He could've pass the "every citizen of Cullen is filthy rich, beautiful, smart and immortal" law and prevent any possible revolt.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How could this guy realize that he could alter reality whenever he wanted and not munchkin the shit out of it?

But still, great story! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this idea. The journal brilliantly shows Ronson's sanity slippage, and the MTF transcripts are very well written. Easy +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There is no more brutal oppression than that encompassed by the phrase 'this is for your own good'. I'm picturing Cullen being somewhere like the town in "It's a Good
Life", wherever it was bixbied to. Instead of being wished into the cornfield, you get a job at the mill.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice post. Great original idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good to see this doing well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep, that's pretty much the point ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good transcripts but the mayor didn't really come out as crazy as MTF pointed him outr to be in them. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Here we go, new article! This is the first article I've written that I've actually planned out in advance, so lets hope it pays off!

Thanks to the following for reading the draft!
chubert
Vacuum
SpookyPizza
DrMagnus
Leveritas

If you want to see more of my stuff, you can find it here!!
